experiment_types: Affinity purification coupled with mass spectrometry proteomics

publications.ref: Hendriks IA, D'Souza RC, Yang B, Verlaan-de Vries M, Mann M, Vertegaal AC. Uncovering global SUMOylation signaling networks in a site-specific manner. Nat Struct Mol Biol. 2014 Sep 14

Characteristics[Label].evidence: Label free quantification confirmed from PRIDE database

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: Using high-resolution mass spectrometry, we have studied global SUMOylation

Characteristics[CellLine].reasoning: PRIDE database indicates HeLa cell line was used, but not explicitly mentioned in this text section
Characteristics[CellLine].needs: Confirmation in methods section or figure legends

Characteristics[Disease].reasoning: PRIDE database indicates cervix carcinoma associated with HeLa cells
Characteristics[Disease].needs: Confirmation if disease context is relevant to the study

Characteristics[Treatment].reasoning: Abstract mentions these treatments for studying SUMOylation dynamics
Characteristics[Treatment].needs: Details on specific compounds, concentrations, and duration

STEP1_OVERVIEW.summary: This is a SUMOylation proteomics study using human cells analyzed by high-resolution mass spectrometry with label-free quantification. The study involves multiple treatment conditions including protease inhibition and heat shock. PRIDE database suggests HeLa cells were used, but this needs confirmation from methods sections. Treatment details and specific compounds require further investigation.

Characteristics[CleavageAgent].evidence: an amount of Sequencing Grade Modified Trypsin (Promega) was added equal to 25% of the Lys-C initially used in a single digestion step. Digestion with trypsin was performed overnight

Characteristics[AlkylationReagent].evidence: Next, chloroacetamide was added to a concentration of 5 mM, and samples were incubated at room temperature for 30 minutes

Characteristics[ReductionReagent].evidence: Subsequently, dithiothreitol (DTT) was added to a concentration of 1 mM, and samples were left to incubate at room temperature for 30 minutes

Characteristics[Label].evidence: Label-Free Quantification (LFQ) Label-Free Quantification was performed using MaxQuant LFQ

Characteristics[Modification].evidence: Cysteine carbamidomethylation was set as a fixed peptide modification

Characteristics[Modification].1.evidence: Protein N-terminal acetylation, peptide N-terminal carbamylation, methionine oxidation, QQTGG and pyro-QQTGG were set as variable peptide modifications

Characteristics[Modification].2.evidence: Protein N-terminal acetylation, peptide N-terminal carbamylation, methionine oxidation, QQTGG and pyro-QQTGG were set as variable peptide modifications

Characteristics[Modification].3.evidence: QQTGG was set as a lysine-specific modification, with a monoisotopic mass of 471.20776

Characteristics[Modification].4.evidence: Pyro-QQTGG (pQQTGG) was set as s lysine-specific modification, with a monoisotopic mass of 454.18121

Comment[EnrichmentMethod].value: His10-SUMO-2 affinity purification with Ni-NTA agarose beads
Comment[EnrichmentMethod].evidence: 20 μL (dry volume) Ni-NTA agarose beads (Qiagen) were prepared per 1 mL lysate, by washing them four times with guanidine Lysis Buffer supplemented with imidazole to 50 mM and β-mercaptoethanol to 5 mM

Comment[Separation].evidence: Separation of peptides was performed using 20 cm long analytical columns (ID 75 μm, Polymicro Avantes) packed in-house with 1.8 μm C18 beads (Reprospher 100)

Characteristics[Modification].5.reasoning: Text mentions additional variable modifications: lysine acetylation and serine-threonine-tyrosine phosphorylation were individually added as further variable modifications in separate searches
Characteristics[Modification].5.needs: Clarification of which specific modifications were used in the final analysis pipeline

Comment[FractionationMethod].reasoning: Text mentions In-solution digested peptides were cleaned, desalted and concentrated on triple-disc C18 reverse phase StageTips, but this appears to be cleanup rather than fractionation
Comment[FractionationMethod].needs: Confirmation whether this constitutes fractionation or just sample cleanup

STEP2_SAMPLE_PREP.summary: Sample preparation involves two-stage His10-SUMO-2 affinity purification using Ni-NTA beads, followed by Lys-C and trypsin digestion. Standard reduction with DTT and alkylation with chloroacetamide were performed. Multiple modifications were searched including fixed carbamidomethylation and variable acetylation, oxidation, QQTGG, and pyro-QQTGG modifications specific to SUMOylation. Label-free quantification was used with C18 reverse phase LC-MS/MS analysis. Additional modifications (acetylation, phosphorylation) were searched separately but their inclusion in final analysis needs clarification.

Comment[Instrument].evidence: The analysis of in-solution digested peptides was performed using an EASY-nLC system (Proxeon) connected to a Q-Exactive (Thermo)

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: The mass spectrometer was operated in data-dependent acquisition mode using a top 10 method

Comment[IonizationType].evidence: prior to online nanoflow liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry

Comment[CollisionEnergy].evidence: HCD tandem MS/MS spectra were acquired using a target value of 1E5, a resolution of 17,500, and a normalized collision energy of 25%

Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance].evidence: MS/MS spectra were filtered for a mass tolerance of 6 ppm for precursor masses

Comment[FragmentMassTolerance].evidence: a mass tolerance of 20 ppm was used for fragment ions

Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages].evidence: Up to 3 missed tryptic cleavages were allowed

Comment[GradientTime].evidence: using a 120 minute gradient from 5% to 75% acetonitrile in 0.1% formic acid

Comment[FlowRateChromatogram].evidence: using a 120 minute gradient from 5% to 75% acetonitrile in 0.1% formic acid and a flow rate of 250 nL per minute

STEP3_MS_SETTINGS.summary: Mass spectrometry instrumentation and acquisition parameters have been fully characterized from the methods section. The study used a Q-Exactive mass spectrometer with HCD fragmentation in data-dependent acquisition mode. MS parameters include 6 ppm precursor mass tolerance, 20 ppm fragment mass tolerance, 25% normalized collision energy, and up to 3 missed cleavages allowed. Chromatography used a 120-minute gradient at 250 nL/min flow rate. All major instrumental parameters required for SDRF annotation have been successfully extracted and confirmed.
